title: Open the Car Door for Me: Teaching an AI MetaHuman to Act, Not Just Talk
excerpt: My second case study takes the AI MetaHuman from a virtual guide that talks and follows to a virtual assistant that acts on your command inside a car showroom.
body:
A few weeks ago I wrote about putting an AI-driven MetaHuman inside a virtual property tour. It was a digital human that could hold a real conversation and follow you from room to room as you explored a home. That was my first case study, and it answered an early question I had: can a virtual human make an immersive experience feel less lonely and a little more human?

Now I am building my second case study, and honestly, this is the part I have been most excited to try. This time the setting is a car showroom, and the MetaHuman is no longer just a guide that talks and follows you around. It can actually do things for you when you ask.

> **The Bigger Picture:** In my first study, the MetaHuman could chat with you and walk beside you. In this one, it can act on what you say. You put on the VR headset, stand next to a car, and say "open the car door for me," and the MetaHuman walks over and opens it. That is the shift I care about: going from a virtual human that only talks to one that also helps.

## From a guide to an assistant

In the property study, anything physical was still on you. The MetaHuman could answer questions, explain a room, and stay by your side, but it could not change anything in the space. If you wanted something to happen, you had to make it happen yourself.

A car showroom asks for something different. Buying a car is hands on. People want to open the doors, sit inside, look under the bonnet, and picture the thing as theirs. So this time I wanted the MetaHuman to feel less like a tour guide and more like a showroom assistant you can actually ask for help.

Here is what the interaction looks like. You are standing next to a car in VR and you say, "open the driver's door for me." The MetaHuman understands what you mean, walks to the right door, and opens it. You did not press a button or point a controller at anything. You just asked, in plain language, and the digital human did it.

## Why this matters

It sounds tiny. A door opens. But the jump underneath that moment is not tiny at all.

For that one small thing to work, the MetaHuman has to pull off a few things at once. It has to keep up a natural conversation like before, understand the intent behind what you said, work out which object in the scene you meant, and then carry out a real action on it. That is the difference between a system that can describe the world and one that can change it for you.

Conversation plus action is what turns a virtual human from a talking presence into something closer to a real assistant. And in a showroom, that lines up almost exactly with what a good salesperson does. They talk with you, they pick up on what you want, and they physically help you experience the product.

## What stays the same, and what changes

The foundation carries over from the first study. Real conversation is still at the heart of it, and the MetaHuman still moves naturally through the space with you. Those were the things I wanted to prove could feel comfortable and believable, and they do.

What changes is agency. The MetaHuman is now taking part in the environment, not just keeping you company inside it. That opens up some genuinely interesting questions:

- Does being able to act on your command make the MetaHuman feel more capable and trustworthy, or does it just raise your expectations to a level that is harder to meet?
- How natural does a spoken command have to be before it feels like talking to a person instead of operating a machine?
- When a digital human physically does something for you, does the whole thing start to feel like a service, and does that change how people engage with the product?

## Why a second industry

Testing the same core technology in a very different setting is on purpose. Real estate and car retail are both big, experience led purchases, but they ask different things of a virtual human. Running the idea across both helps me see what genuinely carries over about AI-driven MetaHumans and what has to be rethought for each context.

If a conversational, action taking MetaHuman can add something real to a home tour and a showroom, that starts to hint at something bigger. It suggests intelligent digital humans could become a general layer of immersive customer experience, rather than a one off trick for a single industry.

## Where this is going

This case study is still in progress, so I am not sharing any results yet. For now it is just the build and the thinking behind it. But the direction feels right. Each step takes the MetaHuman a little closer to something that does not only exist inside a virtual space, but actually helps you while you are in it.

I will keep sharing as it develops. If you work in automotive, retail, or immersive technology and any of this resonates, I would genuinely love to hear how you see it. Feel free to reach out.

title: Would You Trust an AI MetaHuman to Show You Your Next Home?
excerpt: Exploring how AI-driven digital humans could transform property viewing in Virtual Reality.
body:
Imagine buying a home without ever stepping inside it. For many people, particularly international buyers or those relocating for work, this is already a reality. While online listings, photographs, and video walkthroughs have made property searching more accessible, they still leave an important gap: the absence of human interaction.

> **The Bigger Picture:** AI-driven MetaHumans have the potential to transform virtual property tours from passive viewing experiences into interactive conversations. By combining immersive Virtual Reality with intelligent digital humans, future property buyers could explore homes, ask questions naturally, and receive personalised guidance—all within a single virtual environment.

In this blog, I want to explore how AI-driven MetaHumans could bridge that gap by acting as intelligent virtual property guides within immersive Virtual Reality environments.

## The Problem: Property Tours Are Still Limited

The property industry has embraced digital transformation, but much of the viewing experience remains passive. Buyers are often expected to browse through static images or pre-recorded videos, with questions answered later through emails or scheduled calls.

Even live virtual tours can feel restrictive. Once the session ends, so does the opportunity to explore and ask questions.

The experience is informative, but it rarely feels interactive.

## The Opportunity: Bringing AI into Virtual Property Tours

![Metahuman in VR interacting with the user](/assets/uploads/link.png)

Virtual Reality allows potential buyers to walk through a property as if they were physically there. Instead of viewing a room from a fixed camera angle, they can freely explore the environment, inspect details, and experience the property's layout at their own pace.

Now imagine combining that immersive experience with an AI-driven MetaHuman.

Rather than navigating the property alone, visitors could be welcomed by a digital guide capable of interacting naturally throughout the tour. Instead of simply displaying information, the MetaHuman becomes an active participant in the experience.

For example, it could:

- Welcome visitors as they enter the property.
- Answer questions about individual rooms and features.
- Highlight key selling points based on the visitor's interests.
- Provide guidance throughout the virtual tour in real time.

The goal is not to replace human estate agents, but to make virtual property viewing more engaging, informative, and accessible.

---

## From Research Idea to Working Prototype

This vision became the foundation of one of my recent research projects. As part of my ongoing PhD research, I designed and developed an AI-driven MetaHuman integrated into an immersive VR property tour.

The prototype combines several technologies, including:

- Unreal Engine MetaHuman technology for creating a realistic digital human.
- Conversational AI to enable natural interaction.
- A Virtual Reality environment that allows users to explore a property while communicating with the MetaHuman in real time.

Rather than focusing solely on visual realism, the project investigates how conversational intelligence and immersive interaction can work together to improve the overall user experience.

---

## What Did We Learn?

The project represents an early step towards understanding how users interact with intelligent digital humans during immersive property tours.

Our early evaluation explored how participants perceived the experience, particularly in relation to realism, engagement, and interaction quality. While there is still significant work ahead, the initial findings provide encouraging evidence that AI-driven MetaHumans have the potential to make virtual property viewing feel more natural and engaging.

Like any emerging technology, there are also important questions surrounding trust, transparency, and user expectations. These are challenges that will continue to shape my research as the project develops.

---

## Looking Ahead

Although this prototype focuses on real estate, the underlying concept extends far beyond property tours. Similar AI MetaHumans could one day assist visitors in museums, guide patients through healthcare services, support students in virtual learning environments, or welcome customers into immersive retail experiences.

What excites me most is not simply creating realistic digital humans, but designing experiences where AI can communicate naturally and meaningfully with people inside immersive environments.

If you are interested in the technical design, implementation, and early evaluation of this prototype, you can explore the full details in my ACM IMX 2026 Work-in-Progress paper, **AI-Driven MetaHuman for Immersive VR Property Tours: Design and Early Evaluation**.

As always, I would love to hear your thoughts. Would you feel comfortable being guided around a property by an AI MetaHuman, or would you still prefer interacting with a human estate agent?

title: What Happens When AI MetaHumans Start Thinking in Real Time?
excerpt: Inside the Intelligence Layer of Virtual Humans in Immersive Environments
body:
In my previous blog post, I introduced the concept of AI-driven MetaHumans and how they are beginning to reshape immersive brand engagement in Virtual Reality. In this post, I want to take things one step further and explore what actually makes these digital humans feel intelligent in real-time interaction.

Because the real challenge is no longer just about building characters that look realistic, but about designing systems that can think, respond, and adapt naturally as the interaction unfolds.

## Moving Beyond Pre-Defined Behaviour

Most virtual agents and digital characters today still rely heavily on scripted behaviour or constrained decision logic. While this can create a basic sense of interaction, it quickly breaks down when users behave unpredictably or move beyond expected conversational patterns.

AI MetaHumans try to overcome this limitation by moving towards real-time adaptive intelligence. Instead of relying on fixed responses, they generate behaviour dynamically based on context, user input, and environmental cues. This makes the interaction more fluid, less predictable, and closer to natural human communication.

In practice, this means the MetaHuman is not just reacting to isolated inputs, but continuously adjusting its behaviour as the interaction evolves.

## Understanding the Intelligence Layer

In my research, I conceptualise AI MetaHumans as operating through a layered intelligence structure that works together to create the perception of “thinking” within immersive environments.

### Perception Layer

This layer focuses on how the MetaHuman interprets both the user and the environment. It includes:

- Natural language understanding during conversation
- Recognition of user interaction patterns in VR
- Spatial awareness such as gaze direction and movement

Rather than treating inputs as isolated events, this layer builds a continuous understanding of user presence.

### Cognitive Layer

This is the reasoning core of the system, where decisions are formed. It is responsible for:

- Context-aware response generation using AI models
- Maintaining conversational memory across interactions
- Aligning responses with the virtual environment or brand context

This layer ensures that the MetaHuman does not simply respond, but responds with meaning and continuity.

### Expression Layer

Even with strong intelligence, the experience fails if it is not expressed naturally. This layer translates decisions into human-like behaviour such as:

- Facial expressions and micro-expressions
- Body gestures and posture changes
- Voice tone, pacing, and emotional alignment

It is this layer that transforms internal computation into perceived presence.

## Why Real-Time Matters in Virtual Reality?

Virtual Reality fundamentally changes how users experience interaction. Unlike traditional interfaces, users are physically present inside the environment rather than observing from a distance. This creates much higher expectations for responsiveness, realism, and continuity.

Even small delays or unnatural pauses can break immersion almost immediately. Because of this, real-time processing becomes essential. The system must continuously interpret input, make decisions, and generate responses without disrupting the flow of interaction.

In this sense, real-time intelligence is not just a technical requirement, but a core design principle for immersive systems.

## My Current Research Direction

In my PhD research, I am currently developing VR-based AI MetaHuman prototypes using Unreal Engine and  Unity 3D, focusing on real-time interaction systems that combine conversational AI with immersive design principles.

> **The Prototypes:** These prototypes are designed to explore how users respond when interacting with intelligent digital humans in brand-focused virtual environments. The focus is not only on visual realism, but also on how intelligence, behaviour, and interaction design come together to shape user experience.

## Looking Ahead

As this research evolves, the focus is gradually shifting from building visually convincing characters to designing socially intelligent systems that can adapt and respond in meaningful ways.

Future work will explore how trust develops between users and AI MetaHumans, how emotional engagement is shaped in immersive environments, and what ethical considerations emerge when deploying intelligent digital agents in commercial VR spaces.

The boundary between human interaction and artificial presence is becoming increasingly blurred, and we are only beginning to understand its implications.

What do you think makes an AI interaction feel truly real in immersive environments?

title: Beyond the Screen: Why the Future of Branding Belongs to AI MetaHumans
excerpt: Moving beyond static ads to explore how intelligent, AI-driven MetaHumans are completely redefining how we experience and interact with brands in virtual worlds.
body:
Welcome to my very first blog post! If you have explored my portfolio, you will know that my research lives at the intersection of Artificial Intelligence, Virtual Reality (VR), and Human-Computer Interaction (HCI). Specifically, I am focused on a question that will define the next decade of digital experiences: **How can we build AI-driven MetaHumans to transform immersive brand engagement?**

For my inaugural post, I want to pull back the curtain on why this research matters and where the digital world is heading.

---

### The Problem: Traditional Marketing is Losing Its Grip

Let’s be honest—the way brands talk to us online is feeling increasingly dated. Static banner ads, pre-roll videos, and generic social media feeds are struggling to hold our attention. Consumers today don’t just want to view content; they want to **experience** it.

As we transition into fully spatial environments like Virtual Reality and augmented spaces, traditional digital marketing frameworks begin to crumble. You cannot simply paste a 2D billboard into a 3D virtual world and expect it to feel natural.

### The Solution: Enter the AI MetaHuman

My research looks at a much more dynamic alternative: **AI-driven MetaHumans**. These are highly realistic, intelligent digital entities designed to interact with consumers in real time within virtual spaces.

Unlike the rigid, pre-scripted chatbots of the past, true AI MetaHumans combine three critical pillars:

1. **Hyper-Realistic Visuals:** Creating digital humans that look, move, and express emotion authentically.
2. **Contextual AI Intelligence:** Giving these entities the cognitive ability to understand user behaviour, read conversational nuances, and respond intelligently.
3. **Immersive Interaction (HCI):** Optimising how users naturally interact with these virtual beings so the experience feels deeply human, rather than technical.

> **The Goal:** To move from passive consumption to active, emotional collaboration between human consumers and digital brand ambassadors.

### Why This Changes the Game

When an AI MetaHuman can welcome you into a virtual flagship store, understand your unique style preferences, answer complex questions, and exhibit genuine empathy, the relationship with a brand shifts completely.

It turns a transaction into an interactive story. This level of immersion doesn’t just increase brand engagement; it fundamentally redefines consumer loyalty and buying decisions in virtual ecosystems.

![](</assets/uploads/ChatGPT Image Jun 11, 2026, 02_09_52 AM.png>)

---

### Looking Ahead

This is just the beginning of my PhD journey, and I am incredibly excited to share the milestones, experimental breakthroughs, and technical hurdles along the way. In upcoming posts, I will be diving deeper into the specific HCI methodologies I am using and exploring the psychology behind virtual trust.

*Thank you for joining me on this journey. What are your thoughts on interacting with digital humans in the future? Let’s connect on LinkedIn or reach out via email—I’d love to hear your perspectives!*
